title: Remove Complete Button During Feedback Collection to Require User Response
story: As a product administrator, I want the Complete button hidden during the feedback step so that free-tier users must provide feedback (thumbs up/down) before finishing, ensuring we collect usage data for product improvement.
description:
When the feedback form is displayed after clicking Complete, the Complete button itself should be removed so users cannot bypass the feedback step. The feedback form (thumbs up/down + optional recommendations) becomes the only path forward.

If the user cancels the feedback form, the Complete button reappears with the full recommendations view. This approach forces feedback from free-tier users while potentially removing this requirement for paid users in the future.
acceptance_criteria: Complete button is hidden when the feedback form is displayed
Users must interact with the feedback form (thumbs up/down) to proceed
Cancel returns the user to the full view with the Complete button restored
ux_changes: Removed the Complete button during feedback collection. The feedback form is the only exit path. Cancel restores the Complete button and full recommendations view.
implementation_notes: This is a free-tier engagement strategy. The Complete button is conditionally hidden when the feedback form is active and restored on cancel.
comment_summary: Developer implemented on staging. Complete button hides on feedback display and reappears on cancel.
